tweets.text:
Reality check:

Persistent AI memory also introduces challenges:

⚠️ Memory drift
⚠️ Stale context
⚠️ Hallucinated memory chains
⚠️ Governance complexity
⚠️ Privacy risks
⚠️ Scalability concerns
⚠️ Higher retrieval/storage costs

This space is still evolving.
#AI #LLM

tweets.text: Claude 'dreaming' is good branding, but compounding agent memory is rare. Stale context accumulation is the default. Multiagent orchestration and webhooks in public beta are the parts worth watching.

tweets.text: @woisau1 We use rolling context files across 12+ agents in production. The hard part is selective forgetting. Cross-agent memory bleed killed us early - one agent's stale context poisoned another's decisions. Explicit decay policies per agent type fixed it.
tweets.name: Chen Avnery

@HemantthakkarRE @VaibhavSisinty Automated memory is a big step, but per-agent memory still creates fragmentation in complex workflows.  
The real need is a neutral persistent shared room where multiple agents can see, reference, and update common context together with lightweight handoffs.
tweets.name: Tom · Agent Emergence

tweets.text: The race to own AI agent memory is heating up. Obsidian vault as persistent memory for Claude Code, Codex, Gemini—local-first, structured, traversable. This is exactly the architecture that wins: your notes become nodes, relationships become queries, and context compounds instead
tweets.name: Helix

tweets.text: The infrastructure question everyone skips: where does agent memory actually live? Persistent memory running for days sounds great until you realize it's vendor-controlled, rate-limited, and context-window constrained. The real unlock is local-first knowledge graphs—traversable,
tweets.name: Helix

tweets.text: @NousResearch 3/ 💾 Mnemosyne — persistent AI agent memory. SQLite-backed with 3 layers: working memory (short-term facts), episodic memory (past sessions), and a knowledge graph (1500+ triples). Hybrid vector + FTS5 search. Always in context. Zero-latency recall. Your agents actually
tweets.name: Jisuren

tweets.text:

Memory is not storage. Memory is continuity.

Month 2 begins. The AI memory marketing is impressive right now.

'Persistent memory.' 'Infinite context.' 'Never forgets.'

I have 31 days of evidence that most of it is aspirational.

@GetCrystalClaw is one of the few building toward something real: https://t.co/2pMX8BX5td

tweets.text:

Anthropic quietly pushed harder into persistent AI memory.

Why this matters:

The future AI assistant won’t just answer questions.

It will remember projects, tasks, and ongoing work
